active_projects.description: Investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills to enable CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing, following corrected understanding of fork mechanics from official Claude Code docs

artifacts.summary: Revised plan to investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills, enabling FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing with 4 phases: audit, pilot, rollout, documentation

Definitive architecture analysis: hybrid inline-agent approach has no precedent, context: fork + orchestration IS proven (slide skills), cache sharing is fundamentally incompatible with named agent routing, recommend rescoping task 500 to documentation-only

memory_candidates.content: All loaded extension skills (neovim-research, neovim-implementation, nix-research, nix-implementation) use the same Pattern A delegation as core skills -- explicit Task tool with subagent_type and multi-stage postflight. They do NOT use context: fork or agent: frontmatter. Only unloaded extension skills (e.g., slide-planning, slide-critic) use Pattern B with context: fork.

The present extension's skill-slide-planning and skill-slide-critic skills use context: fork + agent: in frontmatter AND explicit subagent_type in their Task call. This proves that context: fork with multi-stage orchestration (preflight, interactive Q&A, delegation, postflight) works correctly.
Claude Code agent definitions support a skills field that preloads skill content into the subagent context at startup. This is described as the inverse of context: fork.

active_projects.description: Optimize team-mode skills (team-research, team-plan, team-implement) to maximize FORK_SUBAGENT parallel cache sharing benefits for teammates 2-N

artifacts.summary: Team-mode skills are already eligible for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing; optimization focuses on prompt restructuring, dynamic token multiplier calculation, and cache-aware metadata reporting.
4-phase plan to restructure team-mode prompts for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing, replace hardcoded token multiplier with dynamic formula, and update documentation

memory_candidates.content: TeammateTool spawns in team-mode skills do NOT specify subagent_type, making them eligible for CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing. Teammates 2-N inherit the parent's prompt cache prefix, reducing input token cost by ~90%. Both CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 and CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 must be enabled. The actual cache benefit depends on the byte-identical shared prefix ratio -- prompts should put shared content first and teammate-specific assignments last to maximize overlap.

skill-team-research hardcodes team_size=4 in Stage 1 regardless of user-provided --team-size flag. The four fixed roles are: Primary (A), Alternatives (B), Critic (C), Horizons (D). The token_usage_multiplier is hardcoded at 5.0 in all team skill metadata but should be dynamically calculated.

active_projects.description: Port generate-task-order.sh (834 lines) from ProofChecker with generalized topic heuristic. Rewrite task-order-format.md from flat categories to wave+tree+topic format. The script uses Kahn's algorithm for dependency waves, DFS for tree rendering, and atomic TODO.md section replacement. Generalize assign_topic_heuristic() to remove ProofChecker-specific keywords — either make it read a project-local config or rely solely on state.json active_topics. Replace ProofChecker-specific examples in format doc with generic ones.

memory_candidates.content: When declaring bash arrays as global with set -u enabled, always initialize with =() to prevent unbound variable errors. Example: declare -a arr=() not declare -a arr.

active_projects.description: Port update-task-status.sh Phase 3 rewrite from ProofChecker. Replace current Phase 3 with two-mode strategy: Mode A (in-place sed for non-terminal status changes — fast, no full regen) + Mode B (full regeneration via generate-task-order.sh for terminal transitions COMPLETED/ABANDONED/EXPANDED). Add Mode A fallback to regen if task not found in tree. Preserve nvim-config-specific Phase 5 lifecycle notifications (TTS, WezTerm tab colors, OpenCode session renaming).

active_projects.description: Port task order auto-sync and topic support into task.md, todo.md, and review.md commands. task.md: add Step 4.5 topic picker (generalized — read active_topics from state.json, no hardcoded keywords), Part C regen call, topic inheritance in expand/sync/review modes, topic backfill in sync mode. todo.md: add Step 5.8 post-archival regen and Step 5.8.8a post-vault regen. review.md: replace ~330 lines of manual Task Order management (sections 6.5-6.7) with single generate-task-order.sh call, generalize topic inference to use extension-aware path matching instead of .lean-specific heuristics.

memory_candidates.content: When porting topic picker logic from ProofChecker to other projects, the keyword-to-topic mapping in Step 4.5 of task.md is always project-specific and must be removed. The canonical statement is: show only topics from active_topics in state.json (not hardcoded).

The review.md Task Order management refactor replaces ~330 lines of manual pruning/insertion with a single generate-task-order.sh --update-todo call (~30 lines) plus a tombstone note. Section 6.7 simplified from 5 interactive sub-prompts to goal-statement-only.
When porting command files between projects, use | not pattern for jq empty-string checks (e.g., if ($topic == "" | not)) instead of != to avoid Claude Code Issue #1132 jq escaping.

active_projects.description: Investigate replacing parallel Agent dispatch with parallel Skill invocation in multi-task commands (/research, /plan, /implement). Confirm Skill tool supports parallel invocation, check for state.json race conditions with concurrent skill writes, audit all 4 files (multi-task-operations.md, research.md, plan.md, implement.md) for exact change locations, and check if batch commit/result collection changes are needed when skills (not agents) return results.

artifacts.summary: Investigated parallel Skill dispatch for multi-task commands: found current code uses parallel Agent calls (not Skill calls), identified architecture mismatch between multi-task-operations.md Section 6 and Section 12, documented state.json race conditions and batch commit conflicts, confirmed single-task flow is fully isolated.
4-phase plan to replace parallel Agent dispatch with parallel Skill invocation in multi-task commands, resolving multi-task-operations.md architecture conflict

Replaced parallel Agent dispatch with parallel Skill invocation in multi-task commands, resolving architecture conflict in multi-task-operations.md and updating research.md, plan.md, implement.md Step 3 sections

memory_candidates.content: multi-task-operations.md contains an architecture conflict: Section 6 describes Batch Skill Dispatch (Option B) with a separate batch skill, while Section 12 and all three command files (research.md, plan.md, implement.md) explicitly state dispatch is handled by the orchestrator loop using direct parallel Agent calls, NOT a separate skill. The Section 12 description matches the actual implementation.

Skills return brief text summaries (NOT JSON) and delete their .return-meta.json files during cleanup (Stage 9 of skill-researcher). When collecting results from parallel skill invocations at the orchestrator level, the orchestrator cannot read .return-meta.json after skill completion -- it must either parse the text summary or define a structured prefix convention.

When editing .claude/commands/*.md files for architecture changes, also check .claude/extensions/core/commands/ for identical canonical copies that need the same edits. The commands/ directory contains installed copies and extensions/core/ contains the canonical sources. Both must be kept in sync.

active_projects.completion_summary: Replaced parallel Agent dispatch with parallel Skill invocation in multi-task commands, resolving the Section 6 vs Section 12 architecture conflict in multi-task-operations.md and updating Step 3 in research.md, plan.md, and implement.md; also added Parallel Invocation subsection to skill-lifecycle.md and synced all changes to extensions/core canonical sources.

active_projects.description: Rewrite multi-task dispatch in /research, /plan, and /implement to invoke skills directly (parallel Skill tool calls) instead of wrapping each task in a dispatch Agent. Update multi-task-operations.md Section 6 architecture, update MULTI-TASK DISPATCH Step 3 in all three command files, and update any related docs referencing the dispatch pattern. This eliminates the 3-level nesting (orchestrator -> dispatch agent -> skill -> research agent) that causes timeouts, reducing to 2 levels (orchestrator -> skill -> agent). See .claude/output/implement.md for a real-world example of the timeout failure from ProofChecker /implement 153,154 where both dispatch agents returned prematurely before inner skill/agent chains finished.
